Class ASTParser

java.lang.Object
ASTParser
public class ASTParser extends Object
Represents a Java program in an Abstract Syntax Tree (AST), utilizing JavaParser's parsing functionality, converting all parts of a Java program to custom objects storing individual classes, methods, statements, expressions, etc.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors

    Description
    ASTParser(String fileName)
    Parses given Java file and reads in all fields, methods, and constructors

  • Constructor Details

    • ASTParser

      public ASTParser(String fileName) throws FileNotFoundException, MalformedURLException
      Parses given Java file and reads in all fields, methods, and constructors
      Parameters:
      fileName - The file name including the file type (should be ".java")
      Throws:
      FileNotFoundException - If the provided file does not exist
      MalformedURLException - If the file is in an unexpected path

  • Method Details

    • hasManualImport

      public boolean hasManualImport()
      Determines if any imports are not wildcard imports (e.g. java.util.Random or java.util.List)
      Returns:
      true if all imports are not wildcard imports, and false otherwise

    • checkRandomCreationInLoop

      public boolean checkRandomCreationInLoop()
      Determines if there are any Random variable declarations or initializations within a loop.
      Returns:
      true if a Random object creation is found inside a loop, and false otherwise

    • testFieldExists

      public boolean testFieldExists(String type, String name, boolean isConstant)
      Tests whether the program contains a field of the exact type and name, and if the field is a class constant, determines if the field has the correct modifiers.
      Parameters:
      type - The declared type of the field
      name - The name of the field
      isConstant - Whether the field should have all modifiers public static final; the field is a class constant.
      Returns:
      true if the field was succesfully found, and if all modifiers are present for class constants. Returns false otherwise.

    • testMethodExists

      public boolean testMethodExists(String returnType, String name, String[] parameterTypes)
      Tests whether the program contains a method of the provided method signature, including its return type, method name, and list of parameters.
      Parameters:
      returnType - The return type of the method
      name - The method name
      parameterTypes - a String array representing the type of each parameter the method accepts. Should be ordered in the expected ordering of the parameters
      Returns:
      true if the method exists, specific to the parameter order given. Returns false otherwise.

    • loadClass

      public Class<?> loadClass() throws ClassNotFoundException, FileNotFoundException, IOException, InterruptedException
      Converts the AST into to a runnable Java class including all changes such as modifying class constants or variables.
      Returns:
      A loaded class object which can be invoked until the parser's class loader is closed.
      Throws:
      ClassNotFoundException
      FileNotFoundException
      IOException
      InterruptedException

    • changeField

      public boolean changeField(String field, String newValue)
      Changes field value specifically at its declaration, given the field name and a new initialization as String.

      The new value passed should follow these guidelines:

      • Primitive type fields may pass in a value simply enclosed by parenthesis. For example:
        public static final int MAX_SIZE = 0;
        Can be initialized to a new value with the following call: 
            changeField("MAX_SIZE", "15");
      • String type fields should include quotation mark literals enclosing the value. For example:
        public String str;
        Can be given an initialization with the following call: 
            changeField("str", "\"Hello!\"");
      • Array type fields can be initialized to new arrays or references to other objects. When initialized to a new array, the new keyword must used for quick initialization. For example:
        public String[] names = new String[]{"Andrew", "Chloe"};
        Can be initialized with the following calls: 
            changeField("names", "new String[]{\"Colton\", \"Parker\"}");
    changeField("names", "altNames"); // Assuming altNames exists and can be read
      • Object type fields can be initialized to new objects or references to other objects. For example:
        public List<Double> list0;
        Can be initialized with the following calls: 
            changeField("list0", "List.of(1.5, 2.0, 2.55)");
    changeField("list0", "list1"); // Assuming list1 exists and can be read
      Parameters:
      field - The name of the field.
      newValue - The String representation for the new initializing expression of the target field.
      Returns:
      true if the field was succesfully changed, and false otherwise.

    • changeField

      public boolean changeField(Map<String,String> fields)
      Changes field value specifically at its declaration, given the field name and a new initialization as String. Changes all field values specifically at their declaration, given a mapping from their name to their new value. The provided mapping should contain keys which represent the field names, whose values are String representations of the expressions to initialize each field to.

      See changeField(String, String) for guidelines on how to represent values as a String.

      Parameters:
      fields - The mapping from each field's name to its new initializing expression.
      Returns:
      true if all fields were succesfully changed, and false otherwise.
      See Also:
      • changeField(String, String)

    • changeVariable

      public boolean changeVariable(String methodName, String[] methodParamTypes, String varName, String newValue)
      Given a variable name and the method it is declared in, replaces the value of the variable with a new value. The new value passed in should be represented as a String, following guidelines defined in changeField(String, String).

      The new value for the variable will be reassigned at the latest write to the variable, prior to the first read within the method.

      For example, for the method: 
          public static void method0() {
        String favoriteColor;
        favoriteColor = "green";
        String randomColor = "red";
        randomColor = favoriteColor = "blue";
        System.out.println(favoriteColor);
    }
      We can change the value of favoriteColor before it is read in the print statement by: 
          changeVariable("method0", new String[0], "favoriteColor", "\"purple\"");
      Which reassigns favoriteColor at the latest assignment, which is at the nested assignment:
      randomColor = favoriteColor = "blue".
      Parameters:
      methodName - The name of the method in which the target variable is declared.
      methodParamTypes - A String[] containing the parameters of the method in which the target variable is declared.
      varName - The target variable name.
      newValue - The String representation for the new initializing expression of the target variable.
      Returns:
      true if the variable was changed, and false otherwise.
      See Also:
      • changeField(String, String)

    • close

      public void close() throws InterruptedException, IOException
      Removes all temporary files created from loadClass().
      Throws:
      InterruptedException
      IOException
